publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 76-79. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest William. I have been quite enchanted this morning, to have both a long letter, & also a message from you by Mr Bray.-- What a sweet mate you are!-- The avis thinks a great deal about ou.--
I find that the beer allowance you make [makes with the s crossed out] is not thought sufficient by the household and have satisfied myself by

enquiry that their complaint is just, & that your rate is too low, & as regards the women absolutely shabby.-- Without telling the Hen anything about our family, I asked her what was a fair beer allowance to give. She replied immediately 3s..6d to the men, 3s..Od to the women, and that she had herself given particular attention to the subject & made many enquiries.-- The Montgomerys who are remarkably economical, say the same. Some people they tell me give only 2s..6d to
the women, but this is thought rather shabby than otherwise.

I am much please with Hewett's conduct on the subject, which has been very unpresuming & sensible, & very different from Thyer's caloric manner the other day.-- As regards us, she is now so good a servant that I only hope her quarrels with other people will not oblige us to part with her.--
I am annoyed at the amount of our house-bills this week, especially the butter which comes to 17s..4d. This is owing to the carelessness of

the dairy-maid last week in only sending 3 lbs, often having had every notice & direction about it.-- This week she has only sent 7 lbs. I desired 9 lbs to come always, & think even this perhaps under the mark.-- The cash came yesterday quite safe.--

There has been another letter from Miss Murray, who is very happy & says "that there is "so little of gossip or even news, "she could fancy it the Happy "Valley".-- The Queen's absence of selfishness & thought for others is quite extraordinary, & she seems to impart a delightful
tone to the whole circle.-- What a promise this is!--
I am amused with Ford's letter, but I hope you will have to announce a son to him. 11 days from this would secure me Dr Locock, but I am afraid I hardly shall manage as much.--
1/2 pst 5 o'clock. Since the above was written, Locke has been here. I had no opportunity of speaking to him alone. He brought excellent accounts. Some of

the conversation between him & Hester annoyed me. It was about the butler at Woburn, who is going, & a new one who is coming. I thought she shewed much repressed petulance, and it turned out Charlotte had been writing to her about it.--
There is some mystery about their proceedings respecting the Woburn servants, which strikes me as very odd.--
